fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity washington, how to transfer occupational therapist license to washington, occupational therapist compact washington, washington occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Washington DOH — Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address: Washington State Department of Health, PO Box 47852, Olympia, WA 98504-7852

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Washington.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create SAW Account & Start Online Application
steps.description: Create or log into your SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account, add the Department of Health Professional Licensing Services, and complete the online Occupational Therapist application form through the DOH portal.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable $100 application fee directly through the SecureAccess Washington (SAW) online portal as part of your application submission.

steps.title: Submit Transcripts and NBCOT Scores
steps.description: Arrange for official academic transcripts, verifying completion of an approved OT program, and your official National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) score report to be sent directly from your educational institution and NBCOT to the Washington State Department of Health.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Submit fingerprints for a state and federal background check as per the instructions provided after you submit your online application. This typically involves using a designated vendor.

steps.title: Request Out-of-State License Verification (Endorsement Applicants Only)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, request verification of all current and past occupational therapist licenses to be sent directly from the issuing state boards to the Washington State Department of Health.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Washington offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Washington DOH — Occupational Therapy for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Washington is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Washington without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Washington also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $141. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Washington have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Washington has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Washington is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Washington without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Washington also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Washington
hero.dek: Occupational Therapists in Washington State apply for licensure through the SecureAccess Washington (SAW) online portal, which provides access to the Department of Health Professional Licensing Services.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license, first create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account (if you don't have one). Then, add the Department of Health (DOH) Professional Licensing Services to your SAW services. Once added, you can access the DOH portal to begin and manage your application.
